summary: I read both design guides and checked the Dashboard UI work under review (queries, services, controllers, views, helpers, Stimulus controllers, tests, and the guide/schema additions) against them, re-verifying every point against the current tree since only `input.json` changed this iteration. The architecture is strongly guide-aligned: business logic sits in reusable query POROs and a `Dashboard::Broadcast` service, controllers stay thin, broadcasts fire from services after commit over a per-user `:dashboard` stream targeting the smallest DOM unit with progress ticks opting out, `status_badge` is the single semantic-color source with the status word always shown, the presentation-boundary rescue is correctly scoped, and Minitest covers queries/service/controllers/system flows — and both genuinely new patterns were added to the guides in the same PR as CLAUDE.md requires. I emitted a `needs_work` verdict with six minor, individually guide-cited deviations (off-scale red-500/green-500 status fills, a hand-rolled attention badge that bypasses `status_badge`, run-selection/JSON logic living in a view helper rather than a PORO, empty states missing the mandated icon/action, the fleet poll fallback departing from stream-first without a guide amendment, and off-scale `mt-0.5`/`py-1.5`/`h-1.5` spacing) — no blockers or majors, and none affects behavior.
